history: The client has recently experienced the deaths of an uncle and a cousin. He is concerned about his father's health, which includes uncontrolled diabetes and other serious conditions. There is tension with his father due to emotional distance and lack of transparency about health issues. The client immerses himself in mathematics as a coping mechanism, finding satisfaction and accomplishment in teaching and understanding complex concepts. He feels self-conscious about his preoccupation with math, using it as a distraction from personal concerns.
intermediate_belief: If I don't stay focused on math, then I will have to confront my father's health issues and our strained relationship.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't solve my family's problems, then I am failing as a son.
coping_strategies: The client immerses himself in mathematics as a distraction from personal issues. He finds solace in the structure and logic of math, which contrasts with the unpredictability of his family dynamics. This intellectual pursuit provides temporary relief from his worries.
situation: Received a phone call from the mother regarding the father's health. Watched television afterward.
auto_thought: I can't handle this right now. I need to escape into something else.
behavior: The client turns to watching television as a means to distract himself from the stress of the phone call. He avoids engaging with the emotional impact of the situation.
